title: Pilosella paniculata
intro: Pilosella paniculata, also known as Rough Hawk's Beard, is a perennial flowering plant in the dandelion family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, inflammation, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella paniculata include sesquiterpene lactones, such as paniculatin, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to treat digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Pilosella paniculata is classified within the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the rose family. This family is composed of flowering plants, with Pilosella paniculata being a specific species within it. Pilosella paniculata is further classified as a member of the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants within the larger family Rosaceae. The genus Pilosella is known for its dandelion-like appearance and is commonly found in Europe and Asia. Pilosella paniculata is often considered a synonym for Hieracium paniculatum, which is a classification that may be used in some botanical references.
uses: Pilosella paniculata has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant has been employed to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations. Its leaves and roots contain compounds that have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Additionally, Pilosella paniculata has been used to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of conditions such as eczema and acne. However, more research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and risks associated with using this plant for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Pilosella paniculata, also known as a type of dandelion, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant contains flavonoids and other compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It is sometimes used to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Some people also use Pilosella paniculata as a natural diuretic and to help lower blood pressure. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.

The plant's extracts have been studied for their ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Additionally, Pilosella paniculata has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's therapeutic potential and to confirm its effectiveness in treating specific diseases. constituents: Pilosella paniculata contains sesquiterpene lactones as its active constituents, which have been reported to have various biological activities. These compounds have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Additionally, Pilosella paniculata also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its pharmacological effects. The exact composition and concentration of active constituents may vary depending on the plant's origin, growth conditions, and preparation methods. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential therapeutic applications of Pilosella paniculata.
parts: Pilosella paniculata has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal parts include the leaves and flowering stems. preparations: Pilosella paniculata is a plant used in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in European folk remedies.  The plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat various health issues.
